steps.title: Identify the punctuation structure
steps.content: An em-dash after “photographs” opens a supplementary element explaining what was photographed. That opening em-dash requires a closing em-dash to bracket the parenthetical and separate it from “to powerful effect in her exhibition.” A comma may also appear inside the em-dashes to separate sub-elements of the supplement.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'described, from hair to grass to sculptures': No closing em-dash — leaves the parenthetical opened by the dash after “photographs” unclosed. Violates paired-dash rule. ✗
  • Choice B — 'described, from hair to grass to sculptures—': Comma separates the two sub-elements inside the parenthetical; closing em-dash pairs with the opening em-dash after “photographs.” Correct. ✓
  • Choice C — 'described—from hair to grass to sculptures,': Places a dash between the two supplementary sub-elements instead of a comma, then ends with a comma instead of a closing em-dash — mismatches the opening em-dash. Violates paired-dash rule. ✗
  • Choice D — 'described: from hair to grass to sculptures': A colon must follow an independent clause; “items they described” ends a relative clause, not an independent clause. Also omits the closing em-dash. Violates colon rule and paired-dash rule. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
